comment_text.sn29.2:1.7: The spontaneous rebirth applies to beings in the heavenly realms, so these dragons would be the powerful lords such as those listed at DN 20:11.1.

root_text.sn29.2:1.4: Aṇḍajā nāgā, jalābujā nāgā, saṁsedajā nāgā, opapātikā nāgā.
root_text.sn29.2:1.5: Tatra, bhikkhave, aṇḍajehi nāgehi jalābujā ca saṁsedajā ca opapātikā ca nāgā paṇītatarā.
root_text.sn29.2:1.6: Tatra, bhikkhave, aṇḍajehi ca jalābujehi ca nāgehi saṁsedajā ca opapātikā ca nāgā paṇītatarā.
root_text.sn29.2:1.7: Tatra, bhikkhave, aṇḍajehi ca jalābujehi ca saṁsedajehi ca nāgehi opapātikā nāgā paṇītatarā.

translation_text.sn29.2:1.2: “Mendicants, dragons reproduce in these four ways.
translation_text.sn29.2:1.4: Dragons are born from an egg, from a womb, from moisture, or spontaneously.
translation_text.sn29.2:1.5: Of these, dragons born from a womb, from moisture, or spontaneously are better than those born from an egg.
translation_text.sn29.2:1.6: Dragons born from moisture or spontaneously are better than those born from an egg or from a womb.
translation_text.sn29.2:1.7: Dragons born spontaneously are better than those born from an egg, from a womb, or from moisture.
translation_text.sn29.2:1.8: These are the four ways that dragons reproduce.”
